input.old_string:
\emph{Step 3: the presheaf homology vanishes locally on the affine basis.} This presheaf is not zero
  as a presheaf, but it vanishes on a basis. Over any basic affine \(D(g)\) small enough to sit inside
  a single cover element \(U_i\), the affine tilde isomorphism
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_isIso_fromTildeGamma}) identifies \(\mathcal{F}|_{D(g)}\) with \(\widetilde{M}\),
  and the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\widetilde{M}\) over the induced standard cover is exact in
  positive degrees: this is precisely Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover} (whose
  section-level exactness packaging is Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}). Since the basic
  affines contained in the cover elements form a basis cofinal among all opens, the canonical map
  \(0 \to (\text{presheaf homology})\) is a local isomorphism --- it is locally bijective.
input.new_string:
\emph{Step 3: the presheaf homology vanishes locally, via the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy.}
  This presheaf is not zero as a presheaf, but it vanishes on a basis. The relevant basis is the family
  of opens \(V\) contained in some single cover element \(U_i\); since \(\{U_i\}\) covers \(X\), every
  open is covered by such \(V\) (intersect with the \(U_i\)), so this family is cofinal among all opens.
  Fix such a \(V\) with \(V \subseteq U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\). The section complex
  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})(V)\) is the {\v C}ech cochain complex of
  \(\mathcal{F}\) over the \emph{restricted} cover \(\{U_s \cap V\}_s\) of \(V\); this restricted cover
  has the member \(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V = V\) equal to the whole of \(V\). A cover possessing a
  member equal to the total space carries a \emph{contracting homotopy}: the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
  map \((h\,s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, i_0 \ldots i_p}\) (with the evident
  restriction along \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \cap V \subseteq U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, i_0 \ldots i_p} \cap V\))
  satisfies \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\). This is the section-level objectwise analogue of the
  combinatorial homotopy \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\) /
  \(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_spec}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}; it is
  \emph{cover-agnostic and coefficient-agnostic} --- it uses neither quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\)
  nor affineness of the cover, only that \(V\) sits inside a cover member. Hence the section complex over
  every such \(V\) is contractible, so its homology vanishes in every degree. Consequently the canonical
  map \(0 \to (\text{presheaf homology})\) is a local isomorphism on this basis --- it is locally
  bijective. (This is a different mechanism from the affine {\v C}ech vanishing of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_cech_vanishing_tilde_subcover}, which concerns the \emph{standard} cover
  \(\{D(f_i)\}\) of an affine and feeds the 02KG Serre vanishing, not the present local check.)

input.old_string:
\emph{Step 4: the augmentation node.} Separately, the augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to
  \mathcal{C}^0\) is the kernel inclusion: the degree-\(0\) homology of the augmented complex vanishes
  because over each basic affine the augmented section complex is exact at degree \(0\). This
  degree-\(0\) exactness is certified by the comb-differential exactness lemma
  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_exact}\), itself a consequence of the spanning-family criterion
  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}\) (a module map into a product of localizations away
  from a spanning family \(\{g_i\}\) is injective with image the equalizer of the two restriction
  maps) --- the same spanning-family argument that gives exactness in positive degrees produces
  exactness at the augmentation node. Sheafifying this local exactness as in Step 3 makes the
  augmentation node homology zero. Together with Steps 1--3, every homology object of the augmented complex is zero, so
  the complex is exact and the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of
  \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\).
input.new_string:
\emph{Step 4: the augmentation node.} The augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to
  \mathcal{C}^0\) is handled by the \emph{same} prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy of Step 3. Over an
  open \(V \subseteq U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the contracting homotopy extends to the augmentation term:
  the degree-\((-1)\)/degree-\(0\) component of \(h\) sends a section \(s \in \prod_s \mathcal{F}(U_s
  \cap V)\) to its \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-component \(s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \in \mathcal{F}(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}
  \cap V) = \mathcal{F}(V)\), so the identity \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\) holds at the
  augmentation node as well. Thus the augmented section complex over each such \(V\) is exact at degree
  \(0\) (and the augmentation is the kernel inclusion there). Sheafifying this local exactness as in
  Step 3 makes the augmentation-node homology zero. Together with Steps 1--3, every homology object of
  the augmented complex is zero, so the complex is exact and the {\v C}ech nerve of
  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\). In
  particular the statement holds for an arbitrary \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) and an
  arbitrary open cover; quasi-coherence and affineness are needed only by the downstream consumers
  (termwise \(f_*\)-acyclicity), not by this exactness.

# Iter-053 objectives

Two deep `mathlib-build` lanes on freshly-scaffolded downstream files (both gate-cleared, both compile
as stubs).

## Lane 1 — `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAugmentedResolution.lean`
- Target: `cechAugmented_exact` (1 stub sorry). Blueprint `lem:cech_augmented_resolution`.
- Route: prepend-`i_fix` homotopy (local vanishing on `{V ⊆ some Uᵢ}`) + `homologyIsoSheafify` +
  `sheafify_kills_locally_zero` + faithful-`toSheaf` IsZero-reflection (3-line helper) across the
  `sheafificationCompToSheaf` square. Recipe: blueprint Steps 1–4 + `analogies/tosheaf-reflect.md`.
- Key build: F-valued objectwise prepend homotopy (template `CombinatorialCech.combHomotopy`).

## Lane 2 — `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/OpenImmersionPushforward.lean`
- Targets: `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_acyclic` (part 1), `higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp`
  (part 2). 2 stub sorries. Blueprint `lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp`.
- Route: presheaf description (01XJ) + `affine_serre_vanishing` (done) for part 1; injective-resolution
  + P4 acyclic-resolution comparison + `f_*∘j_* = (f∘j)_*` for part 2.

## Gate / verification this iter
- blueprint-reviewer `iter053` + `iter053-recheck`: HARD GATE CLEARS both files, 0 must-fix.
- `lake build` GREEN (8331 jobs); only the 3 expected stub-sorry warnings.
- strategy-critic SOUND (CHALLENGE addressed); progress-critic CONVERGING; analogist PROCEED.

8	- leandag uses **statement-level `\uses{}` only**; mirror proof deps into the statement `\uses` or the cone severs. `\uses` takes blueprint labels, not raw Lean names. Cover a helper by bundling its name into a related decl's `\lean{...}` (`private` is NOT exempt from `unmatched`).
9	- KERNEL-SOUNDNESS TRAP (thin-cat coherence, e.g. `pushforwardCongr`/over-Opens H₁/H₂): bare `ext`/`congr 1` auto-close subsingleton-morphism goals with an UNSOUND rfl-term the LSP ACCEPTS but `lake env lean` REJECTS (`unknown free variable _fvar…`). Use explicit `NatTrans.ext`/`congrArg`/`Subsingleton.elim`; ALWAYS re-verify with `lake env lean`+`#print axioms`, never trust the LSP. (plan-validate noop-trap: a 0-sorry objective is DROPPED unless its `.lean`-path LINE carries a literal `_SCAFFOLD_RE` token — `scaffold`/`does not yet exist` [NOT "do not"]/`declarations for`/`add the import`; cost iter-039. Exact list in PROGRESS deferred notes. ALSO: any `## Current Objectives` line containing a `_STOP_MARKER` substring — `do not touch`/`do not assign`/`off-limits`/`do not work on`/`no objective` — is DROPPED whole; cost iter-050 a 0-prover iter. Keep caveats OFF the `### N. **File**` heading line; use heading form + reword as "leave untouched".)
10	- Absolute cohomology = **Form B** `Ext^p_{X.Modules}(jShriekOU U, F)`, `jShriekOU U := sheafify(free(yoneda U))`. The `j_!` FUNCTOR is absent from Mathlib (200–500 LOC), but only the corepresenting OBJECT is needed (~50–80 LOC from `freeYonedaHomEquiv`+`sheafificationAdjunction`); this kills "restriction-preserves-injectives" (SES stays in `X.Modules`, injective in 2nd Ext arg).
11	- `[EnoughInjectives X.Modules]` is NOT absent-and-stuck: it is DERIVED from the frozen target's `[HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]` via a ~6-LOC connector (`InjectiveResolution.instMonoFNatι`). The 01EO/02KG cone carries `[EnoughInjectives]`; build+register the connector at P5b assembly so the cone type-checks.
12	- The free Čech resolution (FreePresheafComplex) is **cover-agnostic**: `𝒰.f` enters ONLY via `coverOpen 𝒰 i := (𝒰.f i).opensRange`; `coverStructurePresheaf := image(cechFreeAug)` uses no `⊤`/`iSup`. So `injective_cech_acyclic` re-parameterizes from `X.OpenCover` to any finite family `(U : ι → Opens X)` with NO covering hypothesis (exact stalkwise on the simplex over `{i:x∈U_i}`). This discharges `BasisCovSystem.injective_acyclic` over covers of any `D(f)` directly — do NOT use `D(f)≅Spec R_f`+restrict (resurrects restriction-of-injectives Form B avoids).
13	- `surj_of_vanishing`: `O_X`-epi ⇒ local-section surjectivity via `Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective`+`Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi'`. `toSheaf` epi-preservation is NOT a small instance — needs `PreservesFiniteColimits(SheafOfModules.toSheaf)` built via the **sheafification square** (`sheafificationCompToSheaf`), NEVER through `forget` (right adjoint = structural wall). Recipe `analogies/tosheaf-epi.md`.
14	- 01I8 `[IsQuasicoherent F]→IsIso F.fromTildeΓ` = **Route B (section-localization)**. Local tiles B0–B4 DONE (per-`gⱼ` global `Presentation(modulesRestrictBasicOpen gⱼ F)`; `QcohRestrictBasicOpen` REUSED, not dormant — only `TildeExactness` is). **KEYSTONE GLUE (iter-041): the span-cover descent on GLOBAL sections (`isLocalizedModule_of_span_cover`) is CIRCULAR** — its per-`gⱼ` hyp is the abstract `LocalizedModule(powers gⱼ)Γ(X,F)`, = keystone-at-`gⱼ`, the same statement (`bridge.md` B6 "section comparison is restrict_obj-rfl" OVER-CLAIMED). Correct route = **sheaf-axiom equalizer** (Stacks 01HV(4), `analogies/keystone-descent.md`): localize F's deg-0/1 sheaf equalizer at `f` (`IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` EXISTS), match vs the `D(f)`-cover equalizer via per-tile localizations (tile lemma ON THE TILDE TILES, never global ΓF), kernel comparison. Sub-lemmas: `qcoh_section_equalizer` [DONE iter-041, more general than blueprinted] / `tile_section_localization` [the LAST leaf] / `qcoh_section_kernel_comparison`. **tile lemma is NOT `restrict_obj`-rfl** (iter-041): `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj` (global ring R) ≠ `Γ(M,-)` (local ring) — carriers not even defeq; needs Sub-lemma B (natural R_g-linear section comparison ~100–150 LOC) + base-ring descent `isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap` (Mathlib LACKS; converse of `of_restrictScalars`). **`IsLocalizing`/`isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff_isLocalizing` DO NOT EXIST** — assembly uses `isIso_fromTildeΓ_iff` (essImage).
15	- `cechAugmented_exact` (augmented Čech complex of qcoh `F` is a resolution): prove exactness via **sections/sheafification**, NOT a stalk functor (`SheafOfModules.stalk` + exact-iff-stalkwise are ABSENT in Mathlib). Route (`analogies/stalkwise-exact-xmodules.md`): reflect `IsZero(homology)` through faithful `toSheaf` (`reflects_exact_of_faithful` — only `Faithful`+`PreservesZeroMorphisms`, both Mathlib instances, NO `PreservesFiniteColimits` needed); `homologyIsoSheafify` ⟹ homology-sheaf = sheafify(presheaf homology); that presheaf is locally zero on the affine basis (`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`) ⟹ vanishes via `LocallyBijective` W-equivalences. The naive "section complex exact over each affine `V`" route is CIRCULAR (= `Ȟᵖ(V,·)`≠0); only the sheafification (local vanishing on refinements `W⊆Uᵢ`) is exact.
16	- A `BasisCovSystem.Cov` MUST carry its covering condition `⨆ᵢUᵢ=D(f)` (NOT all finite basic-open families): an over-broad Cov makes `HasVanishingHigherCech` FALSE for qcoh — `{D(x),D(y)}` on `k[x,y]` has `Ȟ¹(O)≠0` (`x⁻¹y⁻¹∉R_x+R_y`). The covering witness threads from `standard_cover_cofinal`; the field proofs (`injective_acyclic` cover-agnostic, `surj_of_vanishing`) are unaffected. (Distinct from bullet above: that's the `injective_acyclic` FIELD; this is the Cov SET.)
17
